ReaderNote.Note: This reprint properly aligns the material in Schedule 2 of the bill in each official language. Due to a technical issue, this material was not aligned correctly in the original printed version.

Provision.Text: This enactment amends various Acts as part of the Regulatory Modernization Initiative in order to repeal or amend provisions that have, over time, become barriers to innovation and economic growth or to add certain provisions with a view to support innovation and economic growth.
Part 1 modifies the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: replace the requirement to publish a notice of bankruptcy in a local newspaper with a requirement to do so in the manner specified in directives of the Superintendent of Bankruptcy; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide that, if every opposition based solely on grounds referred to in paragraph 173(1)(m) or (n) of that Act is withdrawn, a bankrupt who was eligible for an automatic discharge before the opposition was filed will be issued a certificate of discharge.

It also amends the Electricity and Gas Inspection Act to allow the Governor in Council to authorize the director, appointed under subsection 26(1) of that Act, to establish plans for the verification of meters by any means.

It also amends the Weights and Measures Act to, among other things, enable the Minister of Industry to permit a trader to temporarily use, or have in their possession for use, in trade, any device even if the device has not been approved by the Minister or examined by an inspector.
It also amends the Budget Implementation Act, 2018, No. 2 to, among other things, amend a provision under which certain amendments to the Trademarks Act may be brought into force.

Finally, it amends the Canada Business Corporations Act, the Canada Cooperatives Act and the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act by replacing the term âannual returnâ with the term âannual update statementâ.
Part 2 amends the Canada Oil and Gas Operations Act and the Canada Petroleum Resources Act to repeal certain provisions that require the publication of draft regulations in the Canada Gazette.
It also amends the CanadaâNewfoundland and Labrador Atlantic Accord Implementation Act and the Canada-Nova Scotia Offshore Petroleum Resources Accord Implementation Act to

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: update the terminology in respect of hazardous products in the workplace to ensure alignment and consistency with the Hazardous Products Act; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: clarify the regulation-making authority with respect to record-keeping requirements for occupational health and safety matters.
Finally, it amends the Canada Lands Surveyors Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: enhance the protection of the public by modernizing the complaints and discipline processes that govern Canada Lands Surveyors;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: reduce the regulatory burden of the Minister of Natural Resources by enabling the Council of the Association of Canada Lands Surveyors to make by-laws respecting a broader range of matters;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: harmonize the French and English versions of the Act for consistency and clarity by, among other things, ensuring uniformity between both language versions in relation to the definitions of âlicenceâ and âpermitâ and by addressing certain recommendations of the Standing Joint Committee for the Scrutiny of Regulations;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: improve labour mobility within Canada and to better align with the Canadian Free Trade Agreement; and

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: harmonize the text of that Act with the private law of the provinces and territories, being the civil law regime of the Province of Quebec and the common law regime in the rest of Canada.
Part 3 amends the Wild Animal and Plant Protection and Regulation of International and Interprovincial Trade Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: remove the requirement for the Governor in Council to make and update regulations specifying the animals and plants that are listed as âfaunaâ and âfloraâ, respectively, in an appendix to the Convention on international trade in endangered species of wild fauna and flora; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: clarify that the prohibitions in subsections 6(1) and 7(1) and (2) of that Act are subject to the regulations.
It also amends the Species at Risk Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: authorize the Governor in Council to remove a species from Schedule 3 to that Act if the Committee on the Status of Endangered Wildlife in Canada (COSEWIC) has assessed the status of the species under section 130 of that Act or has determined that the species is not a âwildlife speciesâ or a âspecies at riskâ as defined in subsection 2(1) of that Act;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: remove from that Schedule 3 the species that have already been assessed by COSEWIC under that section 130 or determined by it not to be a âwildlife speciesâ or a âspecies at riskâ as defined in that subsection 2(1);

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: clarify the timelines for preparing proposed recovery strategies and management plans that must be prepared as a result of an assessment under section 130 of that Act; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: repeal Schedule 2 to that Act.
Part 4 amends the Agricultural Products Marketing Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: provide that powers are delegated to a marketing board in relation to the marketing of an agricultural product in interprovincial or export trade by virtue of being named in the schedule to that Act, rather than by Order in Council;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide that the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food is responsible for the delegation of those powers;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: delegate powers in relation to the marketing of agricultural products to administrative bodies;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: provide for limitations and exceptions, that were previously set out in orders and regulations made under that Act, with respect to the exercise of the delegated powers; and

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: require marketing boards and administrative bodies to make accessible to the persons with respect to which they exercise their delegated powers the requirements or other measures they establish in the exercise of those powers.
It also repeals certain Orders and Regulations.
Part 5 amends the Feeds Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: provide that the approval and registration of feed are subject to prescribed conditions and to authorize the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food, to make the approval and registration subject to additional conditions;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide that a notice requiring the removal or destruction of certain feed may be delivered by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: authorize the Governor in Council to make regulations respecting the recognition of a system of any foreign state or subdivision of any foreign state relating to the safety of feeds.
It also amends the Fertilizers Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: provide that the approval and registration of a fertilizer or supplement are subject to prescribed conditions and to authorize the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food, to make the approval and registration subject to additional conditions;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide that a notice requiring the removal or destruction of certain fertilizers or supplements may be delivered by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: prohibit the release of novel supplements, except in accordance with the regulations, and authorize the Governor in Council to make regulations respecting any such release; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: authorize the Minister to impose conditions on any authorization to release a novel supplement that the Minister may grant under the regulations.
It also amends the Seeds Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: provide that a notice requiring the removal or destruction of certain seeds may be delivered by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: prohibit the release of certain seeds, except in accordance with the regulations; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: authorize the Governor in Council to make regulations respecting the release of seeds, providing for the determination of varietal purity of seed crops by the Canadian Seed Growersâ Association and respecting the recognition of a system of any foreign state or subdivision of any foreign state relating to the safety of seeds.
It also amends the Health of Animals Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: provide that a notice requiring the removal or disposal of certain animals or things may be delivered by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: authorize the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food to renew, amend, suspend or revoke a permit or any other document issued by that Minister;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: prohibit the release of certain veterinary biologics, except in accordance with the regulations, and authorize the Governor in Council to make regulations respecting any such release;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: authorize the Minister to approve programs developed by entities other than the Canadian Food Inspection Agency for certain specified purposes and authorize the Governor in Council to make regulations respecting the approval of such programs;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: clarify the circumstances under which an inspector or officer may declare that an infected place is no longer an infected place; and

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: authorize the Minister to make an interim order if the Minister believes that immediate action is required to deal with a significant risk to human or animal health and safety or the environment.
It also amends the Plant Protection Act to

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: provide that a notice requiring the removal or destruction of certain things may be delivered by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: authorize the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food to renew, amend, suspend or revoke a permit or any other document issued by that Minister.

It also amends the Canadian Food Inspection Agency Act to authorize the use of electronic means to administer and enforce that Act and any Act or provision that the Agency is responsible for administering or enforcing.
Finally, it amends the Safe Food for Canadians Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: clarify the definition of âfood commodityâ by specifying that the reference in that definition to the definition of âfoodâ in the Food and Drugs Act is subject to an interpretation provision in that Act;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide that a notice requiring the removal or destruction of certain food commodities may be delivered by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: authorize the Governor in Council to extend any interim order for a period of no more than two years.

Part 6 amends the Pest Control Products Act to, among other things, create a new authorization scheme that will give the Minister of Health the power to authorize pest control products that meet the prescribed criteria, if their risks and value are acceptable and to include mandatory public consultation provisions for this new authorization scheme. The initiative also expands the current authorizations to persons under section 41 and provides for a broader recall power that is applicable to any pest control product that endangers human health or safety or the environment.
Part 7 amends the Coastal Fisheries Protection Act to create an offence of contravening a term or condition of a licence or permit.

It also amends the Fisheries Act to remove the time limit for entry into an alternative measures agreement by an alleged offender and the Attorney General. Finally, it confirms that the provisions respecting alternative measures agreements do not limit the discretion of fishery officers, fishery guardians and peace officers in enforcing that Act.

Part 8 amends the Department of Citizenship and Immigration Act to authorize the Minister of Citizenship and Immigration to disclose, for certain purposes and subject to any regulations, personal information under the control of the Department within the Department and to certain other federal and provincial government entities.

It also amends the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act to authorize the making of regulations relating to the disclosure of information collected for the purposes of that Act to federal departments and agencies.
Part 9 amends the Customs Act to authorize the making of regulations aimed at streamlining the implementation of free trade agreements.

Part 10 amends the Canada Transportation Act to provide the Minister of Transport with the authority to make interim orders to implement international standards or to ensure compliance with Canadaâs international obligations.

Heading.TitleText: Canada Oil and Gas Operations Act
CanadaâNewfoundland and Labrador Atlantic Accord Implementation Act
Canada-Nova Scotia Offshore Petroleum Resources Accord Implementation Act
Wild Animal and Plant Protection and Regulation of International and Interprovincial Trade Act

Heading.Label: PART 5
Heading.TitleText: Regulatory Measures Respecting Canadian Food Inspection Agency
Department of Citizenship and Immigration Act

Provision.Text: Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Subsection 102(4) of the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The trustee shall, as soon as possible after the bankruptcy and not later than the fifth day before the first meeting of creditors, publish, in the manner specified in the directives of the Superintendent, a notice in the prescribed form.

Section.Text: Paragraph 155(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a notice of the bankruptcy shall not be published under subsection 102(4) unless it is deemed expedient by the trustee or ordered by the court;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 170.1:

Subsection.Text: If a creditor or the trustee has opposed the discharge of a bankrupt individual solely on grounds referred to in either one or both of paragraphs 173(1)(m) and (n), the creditor or trustee, as the case may be, may withdraw the opposition at any time before the hearing referred to in subsection 170.1(3).
The following provisions apply in respect of withdrawals under subsection (1):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a creditor withdraws their opposition, they shall give notice of the withdrawal to the Superintendent, the trustee, the bankrupt and, if the mediation is ongoing, the mediator; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the trustee withdraws their opposition, they shall give notice of the withdrawal in the prescribed form and manner to the Superintendent, the bankrupt and, if the mediation is ongoing, the mediator.

Subsection.Text: If all oppositions based solely on grounds referred to in either one or both of paragraphs 173(1)(m) and (n) are withdrawn before the mediation referred to in section 170.1 has begun and the Superintendent, the trustee and a creditor have not opposed the automatic discharge based on any other ground, the trustee shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: withdraw the application for mediation by sending a notice of the withdrawal in the prescribed form to the official receiver, if such an application has been sent to the official receiver under subsection 170.1(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: without delay, issue to the bankrupt a certificate of discharge in the prescribed form releasing the bankrupt from their debts, other than those referred to in subsection 178(1), and send a copy of it to the Superintendent.

Subsection.Text: If all oppositions based solely on grounds referred to in either one or both of paragraphs 173(1)(m) and (n) are withdrawn after the mediation referred to in section 170.1 has begun, but before a hearing under subsection 170.1(3) has begun, and the Superintendent, the trustee and a creditor have not opposed the automatic discharge based on any other ground,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the mediation is ongoing, the mediator shall cancel the mediation by sending a notice of cancellation in the prescribed form to the Superintendent and to the parties;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the trustee shall withdraw the application for a hearing, if such an application has been made under subsection 170.1(3); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the trustee shall, without delay, issue to the bankrupt a certificate of discharge in the prescribed form releasing the bankrupt from their debts, other than those referred to in subsection 178(1), and send a copy of it to the Superintendent.

Section.Text: Paragraph 28(1)(d) of the Electricity and Gas Inspection Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: authorizing the director to establish plans for the verification and reverification â using any means, including sampling â of meters of any class, type or design, or in any circumstances;

Section.Text: The portion of section 8 of the Weights and Measures Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Approval, examination and certification of devices
Section.Text: Subject to section 8.1, no trader shall use, or have in their possession for use, in trade, any device unless
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 8:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may permit a trader to use, or have in their possession for use, in trade, any device on a temporary basis for a period and under any terms and conditions that the Minister may specify, even if the device, or the class, type or design of the device, has not been approved under section 3 or the device has not been examined by an inspector.
The Minister may, by written notice, revoke the permission for failure to comply with any of the terms or conditions on which the permission was granted.
If the permission is revoked, the trader shall not use, or have in their possession for use, in trade, the device that was permitted to be used under the permission.
The Minister shall not revoke the permission unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: notice of the proposal to revoke it has been given to the trader;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any interested person objecting to the proposal has been afforded reasonable opportunity to make representations with respect to their objection; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the representations, if any, have been taken into account in deciding whether to implement the proposal.

Section.Text: Paragraph 10(1)(t) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 26(2) of the Act is repealed.
The Budget Implementation Act, 2018, No. 2 is amended by adding the following after section 229:

Subsection.Text: Subsection 70(1) of the Act is amended by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: sections 36.1, 38.1 and 45.1 and subsection 56(5), as enacted by the Budget Implementation Act, 2018, No. 2.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 70(1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 9(3) and (4), sections 36.1, 38.1 and 45.1 and subsection 56(5), as enacted by the Budget Implementation Act, 2018, No. 2.

Section.Text: Subsection 239(4) of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 242(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Sections 215 and 216 and subsection 229.1(2) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, but that day must be after the day fixed under subsection (2.1).
Sections 217, 219, 221 to 223 and 226 to 228 and subsection 229.1(1) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Section 242 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
Section 225 comes into force on the day on which An Act respecting regulatory modernization receives royal assent.

Section.Text: Section 263 of the Canada Business Corporations Act is replaced by the following:
Every corporation shall send to the Director an annual update statement in the form and within the period that the Director fixes.
Section 374 of the Canada Cooperatives Act is replaced by the following:
Every cooperative must send the Director an annual update statement in the form and within the period that the Director fixes.
Section 278 of the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act is replaced by the following:
Every corporation shall send to the Director an annual update statement in the form and within the period that the Director fixes.

Subsection.Text: Sections 1 and 2 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Section 3 comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Sections 12 to 14 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Heading.TitleText: Canada Oil and Gas Operations Act

Section.Text: Section 15 of the Canada Oil and Gas Operations Act is repealed.
Subsections 107(2) and (3) of the Canada Petroleum Resources Act are repealed.

Heading.TitleText: CanadaâNewfoundland and Labrador Atlantic Accord Implementation Act

Subsection.Text: The definition hazardous substance in subsection 205.001(1) of the CanadaâNewfoundland and Labrador Atlantic Accord Implementation Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: hazardous substanceâincludes a hazardous product and any chemical, biological or physical agent that, by reason of a property that the agent possesses, is hazardous to the health or safety of an individual who is exposed to it.â(substance dangereuse)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 205.001(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
In this Part, hazardous product, label and safety data sheet have the same meanings as in section 2 of the Hazardous Products Act.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 205.022(c) to (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: ensure that all hazardous substances at the workplace, other than hazardous products, are identified in the manner that is prescribed;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subject to the Hazardous Materials Information Review Act, ensure that each hazardous product at the workplace or each container at the workplace that contains a hazardous product has a label that meets the requirements set out in the regulations made under subsection 15(1) of the Hazardous Products Act affixed to it, printed on it or attached to it, in a manner that meets the requirements set out in the regulations made under that subsection;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subject to the Hazardous Materials Information Review Act, make available to every employee at the workplace, in the manner that is prescribed, a safety data sheet that meets the requirements set out in the regulations made under subsection 15(1) of the Hazardous Products Act with respect to each hazardous product to which the employee may be exposed;

Section.Text: Section 205.023 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Employer to provide information in emergency

Subsection.Text: Every employer shall, in respect of each workplace under its control, and in respect of an activity performed by any of its employees at a workplace that is not under its control, to the extent that it controls the activity, provide, in respect of any hazardous product to which an employee may be exposed, as soon as the circumstances permit, the safety data sheet referred to in paragraph 205.022(e) to any physician, or other medical professional that is prescribed, who requests that safety data sheet for the purpose of making a medical diagnosis of, or rendering medical treatment to, an employee in an emergency.

If there is no safety data sheet for the hazardous product referred to in subsection (1), the employer shall provide any information about that product that is in their possession to the physician or other medical professional.

Any physician or other medical professional to whom information is provided by an employer under subsection (1) or (2) shall keep confidential any information specified by the employer as being confidential, except for the purpose for which it is provided.

Section.Text: Subsection 205.086(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Information on hazardous products and materials
Subsection.Text: Information that, under the Hazardous Materials Information Review Act, a person is exempt from disclosing under paragraph 205.022(d) or (e) or under the Hazardous Products Act and that is obtained by a health and safety officer who enters a place under subsection 205.073(3), or by an individual accompanying or a person assisting the officer, is privileged and, despite the Access to Information Act or any other Act or law, shall not be disclosed to any other person except for the purposes of this Part, or for the purposes of Part III as it relates to safety.

Section.Text: Paragraph 205.124(1)(p) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (o.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the records to be kept by an operator or an employer, or both, the manner and form in which and the period of time for which records are to be kept;

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner and form in which information is to be communicated;

Heading.TitleText: Canada-Nova Scotia Offshore Petroleum Resources Accord Implementation Act

Subsection.Text: The definition hazardous substance in subsection 210.001(1) of the Canada-Nova Scotia Offshore Petroleum Resources Accord Implementation Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: hazardous substanceâincludes a hazardous product and any chemical, biological or physical agent that, by reason of a property that the agent possesses, is hazardous to the health or safety of an individual exposed to it.â(substance dangereuse)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 210.001(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
In this Part, hazardous product, label and safety data sheet have the same meanings as in section 2 of the Hazardous Products Act.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 210.022(c) to (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: ensure that all hazardous substances at the workplace, other than hazardous products, are identified in the manner that is prescribed;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subject to the Hazardous Materials Information Review Act, ensure that each hazardous product at the workplace or each container at the workplace that contains a hazardous product has a label that meets the requirements set out in the regulations made under subsection 15(1) of the Hazardous Products Act affixed to it, printed on it or attached to it, in a manner that meets the requirements set out in the regulations made under that subsection;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subject to the Hazardous Materials Information Review Act, make available to every employee at the workplace, in the manner that is prescribed, a safety data sheet that meets the requirements set out in the regulations made under subsection 15(1) of the Hazardous Products Act with respect to each hazardous product to which the employee may be exposed;

Section.Text: Section 210.023 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Employer to provide information in emergency

Subsection.Text: Every employer shall, in respect of each workplace under its control, and in respect of an activity performed by any of its employees at a workplace that is not under its control, to the extent that it controls the activity, provide, in respect of any hazardous product to which an employee may be exposed, as soon as the circumstances permit, the safety data sheet referred to in paragraph 210.022(e) to any physician,âor other medical professional that is prescribed, who requests that safety data sheet for the purpose of making a medical diagnosis of, or rendering medical treatment to, an employee in an emergency.

If there is no safety data sheet for the hazardous product referred to in subsection (1), the employer shall provide any information about that product that is in their possession to the physician or other medical professional.

Any physician or other medical professional to whom information is provided by an employer under subsection (1) or (2) shall keep confidential any information specified by the employer as being confidential, except for the purpose for which it is provided.

Section.Text: Subsection 210.087(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Information on hazardous products and materials
Subsection.Text: Information that, under the Hazardous Materials Information Review Act, a person is exempt from disclosing under paragraph 210.022(d) or (e) or under the Hazardous Products Act and that is obtained by a health and safety officer who enters a place under subsection 210.074(3), or by an individual accompanying or a person assisting the officer, is privileged and, despite the Access to Information Act or any other Act or law, shall not be disclosed to any other person except for the purposes of this Part, or for the purposes of Part III as it relates to safety.

Section.Text: Paragraph 210.126(1)(p) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (o.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the records to be kept by an operator or an employer, or both, the manner and form in which and the period of time for which records are to be kept;

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner and form in which information is to be communicated;

Subsection.Text: The definitions boundary, Canada Lands Surveyor, commission, licence and permit in section 2 of the Canada Lands Surveyors Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: boundaryâmeans a natural or artificial line on the surface of the earth, below the surface of the earth, in airspace or in or on any structure, the purpose of which is to indicate the extent of a legal interest or right in land, land under water, water, airspace or natural resources, or in a structure. â(ligne de dÃ©marcation)
Canada Lands Surveyorâmeans a member who holds a commission.â(arpenteur des terres du Canada)
commissionâmeans a commission granted under section 49.â(brevet)
licenceâmeans a licence issued under section 53.â(licence)
permitâmeans a permit issued under section 58.â(permis)

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Council member means a member of the Council of the Association referred to in section 13. (conseiller)
memberâmeans a member of the Association whose membership is granted under section 38. (membre)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 6(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to establish and maintain standards of qualifications and practice for Canada Lands Surveyors;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 6(c) and (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to establish and maintain standards of conduct, knowledge and skill among members and permit holders;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to govern the activities of members and permit holders;

Section.Text: Section 8 of the Act is replaced by the following:
In carrying out its objects, the Association has the capacity and the rights, powers and privileges of a natural person.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 13(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: members, in a number set out in the by-laws, which number may not be less than three, who are elected in accordance with the by-laws;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 13(e) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: two persons who are not members and who are appointed by the Minister.

Section.Text: Subsection 16(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A vacancy on the Council caused by the death, resignation, removal or inability to act of a Council member referred to in paragraph 13(c) shall be filled by a member in the manner set out in the by-laws.

Section.Text: Section 17 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (2), a Council member appointed under paragraph 13(e) continues to hold office until they are reappointed or their successor is appointed.
Subsection 18(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Council may make by-laws respecting any matter necessary to carry on the business and activities of the Association, including by-laws respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a code of ethics for professional conduct;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the composition of committees established under subsection 19(2) and the rules, practices and procedures of those committees;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the maintenance of records by the Association in respect of members and permit holders, and the inspection of those records;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: applications for membership in the Association and the renewal and reinstatement of memberships in the Association;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: applications for admission as a candidate for a commission, the requirements of candidates, the cancellation of a candidacy, appeals of decisions, the granting of commissions, and the use of the titles âCanada Lands Surveyorâ and âarpenteur des terres du Canadaâ;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the examination of candidates for a commission;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: applications for a licence and permit and their issuance and renewal, and the reinstatement of a licence and permit;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the terms and conditions under which members and their employers must have insurance against professional liability;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the exemption of members and their employers from the requirement set out in paragraph (h);

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the notification of the Registrar, by members and permit holders, of claims against them for professional liability;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: conflict of interest in respect of surveying activities, including the definition of what constitutes a conflict of interest under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: the issuing and ownership of seals, the certification of documents and drawings by members, and the form of statements of responsibility, seals and signatures;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: the procedures to be followed by the Association in reviewing the surveying activities of members and permit holders to ensure the maintenance of minimum standards of surveying; and
any other matter for which by-laws are required by this Act.

Subsection.Text: Section 18 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to by-laws.
By-laws shall be made available to the public in both official languages.
Subsection 21(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Sous rÃ©serve de lâarticle 22, le comitÃ© dâexamen rÃ©git toute question relative Ã lâexamen et Ã lâadmission des candidats au brevet ainsi que les qualifications exigÃ©es dâeux.
Subsection 21(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Board of Examiners may admit a person to be a candidate for a commission only if the person has complied with the requirements specified in the by-laws.

Text.Ins: All matters relating to the admission, qualifications and examination of candidates for a commission who are entitled to survey lands in a province and under the laws of the province and who seek to become licence holders under any provision of this Act, the regulations and the by-laws, are to be conducted in accordance with the provisions of the Agreement, as defined in section 2 of the Canadian Free Trade Agreement Implementation Act.

Section.Text: Section 23 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Board of Examiners shall recommend to the Council that a commission be granted to every candidate for a commission who has complied with the requirements specified in the by-laws.
Sections 24 and 25 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Complaints Committee shall consider all complaints in writing that are received by the Association regarding the conduct or actions of any member or permit holder.

If the Committee has reasonable grounds to believe that a member or permit holder against whom a complaint is made has committed an act of professional misconduct or was incompetent, the Committee may conduct an investigation of the surveying activities of the member or permit holder or designate a person or entity to conduct the investigation under the Committeeâs direction.
The Committee may revoke the designation.

Text.Ins: For greater certainty, an individual appointed to the Committee may be designated to conduct the investigation.
For the purposes of sections 24.1 to 24.5, designated person means the Committee or the person or entity designated by it to conduct the investigation.

Section.MarginalNote: Power to require information and documents

Subsection.Text: The designated person may, for the purpose of investigating a member or permit holderâs conduct and activities, require any member or permit holder

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to provide any information that, in the opinion of the designated person, the member or permit holder is able to provide in relation to the matter being investigated; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to produce, for examination or copying by the designated person, any document or other thing that, in the opinion of the designated person, is relevant to the matter being investigated and may be in the possession or under the control of that member or permit holder.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection 24.2(1), a designated person may, for that purpose, enter the business premises of the member or permit holder in which they have reasonable grounds to believe that an activity regulated under any provision of this Act, the regulations or the by-laws is conducted or any document or other thing relevant to the investigation is located.
The designated person may, for that purpose,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: examine anything in the place;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: remove the thing for examination or copying;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: use any copying equipment in the place, or cause it to be used;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: open or order any person to open any container or package found in the place;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: use any means of communication in the place, or cause it to be used;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: use any computer system or device in the place, or cause it to be used, to examine data contained in or available to it;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: prepare a document, or cause one to be prepared, based on the data; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: take photographs or make recordings or make a copy of anything in the place.

Subsection.Text: The owner or person in charge of the place and every person in the place shall give all assistance that is reasonably required to enable the designated person to perform their functions under subsections (2) and (3) and shall provide any documents or information, and access to any data, that may be reasonably required by the designated person.
If the place is a dwelling-house, the designated person may enter it without the occupantâs consent only under the authority of a warrant issued under subsection (2).

On ex parte application, a justice of the peace may issue a warrant authorizing the designated person to enter a dwelling-house, subject to any conditions specified in the warrant, if the justice is satisfied by information on oath that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the dwelling-house is a place referred to in subsection 24.1(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: entry to the dwelling-house is necessary for the purposes of the investigation; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: entry was refused by the occupant or there are reasonable grounds to believe that entry will be refused by, or that consent to entry cannot be obtained from, the occupant.

Section.Text: In executing a warrant to enter a dwelling-house, the designated person may use force only if the use of force has been specifically authorized in the warrant and they are accompanied by a peace officer.

A person or entity that exercises powers or performs duties or functions under any of sections 24 to 24.3 does not incur civil liability in respect of anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in the exercise of those powers or the performance of those duties or functions.

Subsection.Text: In the course of the investigation, the designated person may require a member or permit holder to respond to any request for information in respect of the complaint within the prescribed period.

If the member or permit holder fails to respond to the request for assistance or information within the prescribed period, they commit an act of professional misconduct. If such is the case, the Complaints Committee may, on its own initiative or on the recommendation of the designated person, refer a complaint respecting that act of professional misconduct to the Discipline Committee in accordance with paragraph 25(1)(b), and in the case of the member who is the subject of that complaint, may suspend that memberâs licence in accordance with section 27.1.
After having considered a complaint, the Complaints Committee shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: dismiss the complaint if it is satisfied that the evidence disclosed by the investigation does not support a finding of professional misconduct or incompetence;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: refer the complaint to the Discipline Committee if it is satisfied that there is sufficient evidence disclosed by the investigation that could support a finding of professional misconduct or incompetence; or

Text.Ins: take any action that it considers appropriate in the circumstances to resolve the complaint.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice of dismissal, referral or resolution
Subsection.Text: The Registrar shall cause a notice of the dismissal, referral or resolution of the complaint, as the case may be, to be served on the Association, the complainant and the member or permit holder who is the subject of the complaint.

Section.Text: Section 26 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Le comitÃ© des plaintes nâa pas Ã tenir dâaudience ou Ã donner Ã quiconque la possibilitÃ© de prÃ©senter des observations orales avant de formuler sa recommandation Ã lâÃ©gard de la plainte.
Section 27 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Complaints Committee retains jurisdiction to deal with a complaint regarding the conduct or actions of a person or entity who has ceased to be a member or permit holder.

Subsection.Text: The Complaints Committee may suspend the licence belonging to the member who is the subject of the complaint, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the continuation of the memberâs surveying activities may put the public at risk; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the complaint was referred to the Discipline Committee in accordance with subsection 24.5(2).

Subsection.Text: A decision of the Complaints Committee to suspend a licence under this section shall be in writing and set out reasons.

The Complaints Committee shall cause a copy of every decision to suspend under this section to be given to the Discipline Committee, the Council and the Registrar. The Registrar shall cause a notice of every decision to suspend under this section to be served on the member who is the subject of the complaint and on the complainant and shall notify the other members of the suspension.
The Registrar shall make public a decision to suspend a memberâs licence under this section.

The decision to suspend is enforceable on being served on the subject of the complaint and remains in force until the Registrar causes a decision of the Discipline Committee regarding the complaint that led to the suspension to be served on the member to whom the decision relates, in accordance with subsection 31(3), and on the Complaints Committee.

A member whose licence has been suspended may, by filing a written notice of appeal within 30 days after the day on which a notice of a decision is served, appeal the decision to suspend the memberâs licence to the Federal Court.

If the memberâs licence was suspended under paragraph 27.1(1)(b), the Discipline Committee may, at the memberâs request, end the suspension if it is of the opinion that the member has responded to the request for assistance or information since the suspension of their licence.

If the Discipline Committee does not end the suspension of the memberâs licence, the member may, by filing a written notice of appeal within 30 days after the day on which the Discipline Committee decides not to end the suspension, appeal the decision to the Federal Court.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 28(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subject to sections 29 and 30.1, hear and determine allegations of professional misconduct or incompetence in complaints submitted to it by the Complaints Committee; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: hear and determine matters referred to it by the Registrar with respect to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the reinstatement of a member in the Association, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the issuance of a new licence or permit to any person or entity whose licence or permit has been revoked.

Section.Text: Sections 29 and 30 of the Act are replaced by the following:

The Discipline Committee shall begin the process of examining a complaint referred to them regarding a suspension under section 27.1 no later than 15 days after the day on which the complaint is referred.
The Discipline Committee retains jurisdiction to deal with a complaint regarding the conduct or actions of a person or entity who has ceased to be a member or permit holder.

The Discipline Committee may, without holding a discipline hearing, take one or more of the actions set out in subsection 31(1) against the member or permit holder who is the subject of the complaint if the member or permit holder

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: agrees with the findings of the Complaints Committee;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: accepts to forego a discipline hearing; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: accepts the actions that the Discipline Committee proposes to take against them.

Section.Text: At a hearing, the Discipline Committee shall provide the member or permit holder who is the subject of the complaint with an opportunity to present evidence and make representations.

Text.Ins: The Discipline Committee may summon and enforce the appearance of persons and compel them to give oral or written evidence on oath and to produce any document or other thing that the Committee considers necessary for the full examination of the complaint, in the same manner and to the same extent as a superior court of record.

Subsection.Text: Discipline Committee hearings shall be public.

However, the Committee may conduct hearings in camera if it considers it necessary to prohibit the disclosure, publication or distribution of information or documents, for a reason of public order, including to ensure respect for professional secrecy or the protection of a personâs privacy, safety or reputation.
The portion of subsection 31(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
If the Discipline Committee finds a member or permit holder guilty of professional misconduct or incompetence, the Discipline Committee may take one or more of the following actions:
Paragraph 31(1)(a) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: annuler lâadhÃ©sion du membre Ã lâAssociation;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 31(1)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: revoke the memberâs commission or licence or the permit holderâs permit;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: suspend the memberâs commission or licence or the permit holderâs permit for a period not exceeding two years;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 31(1)(d) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: accept an undertaking that limits the memberâs or permit holderâs surveying activities to the extent specified in the undertaking;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 31(1)(f) and (g) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: impose on the member or permit holder a penalty not in excess of the amount prescribed by regulation, payable to the Association;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: reprimand the member or permit holder and direct that the reprimand be recorded in the register maintained by the Registrar;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 31(1)(h) and (i) of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: direct the member or permit holder to reimburse the complainant, the Association or both all or any part of the costs associated with the complaint;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: direct that the members be notified of any action taken by the Discipline Committee under this section; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 31(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Discipline Committee shall cause a copy of every decision to take action under this section to be given to the Council and the Registrar, and the Registrar shall cause a copy of the decision to be served on the member or permit holder to whom the decision relates and on the complainant.
Subsection 31(4) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Sâil est dâavis que les circonstances le justifient, le comitÃ© de discipline peut, selon les modalitÃ©s quâil prÃ©cise, suspendre lâexÃ©cution de sa dÃ©cision pour la pÃ©riode quâil estime appropriÃ©e.
Subsection 31(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Registrar shall notify the members whenever a membership in the Association is cancelled or a commission, licence or permit is revoked or suspended.
The Registrar shall make public a decision of the Discipline Committee to cancel a memberâs membership in the Association or to revoke or suspend a memberâs commission, licence or permit.
Subsections 31(6) and (7) of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the Discipline Committee finds that an allegation of professional misconduct or incompetence against a member or permit holder is unfounded, the Registrar shall, on the request of the member or permit holder, notify the members of that finding.

If the Discipline Committee is of the opinion that proceedings before it in relation to any member or permit holder are unwarranted, it may recommend to the Council that the Association reimburse the whole or part of the memberâs or permit holderâs costs.

Section.Text: Section 34 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Registrar shall maintain a register in which is recorded the name of every Canada Lands Surveyor and all other information required by the by-laws to be recorded in that register.
The Registrar shall maintain a register in which is recorded

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name and address of every member who holds a licence, every other member and every permit holder;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information that is required by the by-laws to be recorded in that register;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: information that is directed by the Discipline Committee to be recorded; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: information that is directed by the Council to be recorded.

Section.Text: Subsection 35(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Registrar shall provide to any person, on payment of a reasonable charge, a copy of any part of a register pertaining to any member.

Section.Text: Section 37 of the Act is replaced by the following:

No action or other proceedings for damages shall be instituted against the Association, a committee of the Association, a member, an individual appointed to a committee of the Association or an officer, employee, representative or appointee of the Association for any act done in the performance of any duty, or the exercise of any power, in good faith, under this Act or for any neglect or default in the performance of any duty, or the exercise of any power, in good faith, under this Act.
Section 38 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Registrar shall grant membership in the Association to any person who holds a commission, or to any other person specified in the by-laws, who applies for membership and who complies with the regulations and the by-laws.
Sections 39 and 40 of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

The Registrar may cancel a membership in the Association for the non-payment of any fee or levy prescribed in the by-laws after giving the member at least two monthsâ written notice of the default and intention to cancel.
A member may resign from the Association by filing a resignation in writing with the Registrar.
Subsection 41(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person whose membership in the Association has been cancelled for professional misconduct or incompetence may, any time after two years after the date of the cancellation, apply in writing to the Registrar, in accordance with the by-laws, to be reinstated as a member.

Section.Text: Section 42 of the Act is replaced by the following:
A member who directs the provision of surveying services is in all respects liable for maintaining the standards of conduct and competence in respect of the provision of those services.

Section 47 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Members shall certify their documents and drawings in accordance with the by-laws.
Section 48 of the Act is repealed.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 49:

Text.Ins: A person, other than a member who holds a commission, shall not use the title âCanada Lands Surveyorâ or âarpenteur des terres du Canadaâ, or any addition to or abbreviation of that title, or any words, name or designation that leads to the belief that the person is a Canada Lands Surveyor.

Section.Text: Section 50 of the French version of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:
Seul le titulaire dâune licence ou une personne agissant sous sa direction peut effectuer lâarpentage cadastral des terres du Canada ou de terrains privÃ©s dans un territoire.
Section 51 of the Act is replaced by the following:
A member, other than a member who is a licence holder or who is acting under the direction of a licence holder, shall not engage in surveying on Canada Lands or private lands in a territory.

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 52 of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection 21(3), have experience and practical training in surveying totalling at least two years during the previous five years; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection 21(3), comply with the licensing requirements prescribed by the by-laws.

Section.Text: Section 53 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Registrar may issue a licence to any person who applies for a licence and complies with the requirements under this Act.
Section 54 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection 55(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person whose licence has been revoked for professional misconduct or incompetence may, any time after two years after the date of the revocation, apply in writing to the Registrar, in accordance with the by-laws, for a new licence.
Subsection 56(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsection (2) and the by-laws, every member who engages in surveying must be insured against professional liability.
Subsection 56(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of a member who is employed by Her Majesty in right of Canada or a province, by an agent of Her Majesty in right of Canada or an agent or mandatary of Her Majesty in right of a province.
Subsection 56(4) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section 58 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Registrar may issue a permit to an entity that applies for a permit in accordance with the by-laws.
Section 59 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Le registraire peut annuler le permis pour dÃ©faut de paiement des droits ou cotisations fixÃ©s par les rÃ¨glements administratifs, aprÃ¨s avoir donnÃ© au titulaire un prÃ©avis dâau moins deux mois entre le non-paiement et lâÃ©ventuelle annulation.
Section 60 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Registrar may revoke a permit if the permit holder no longer has a director, officer, partner or employee who holds a licence and who is capable of personally supervising and directing cadastral surveys.
Subsection 61(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An entity whose permit has been revoked for professional misconduct or incompetence may, any time after two years after the date of the revocation, apply in writing to the Registrar, in accordance with the by-laws, for a new permit.
The portion of section 62 of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: The Council may, with the approval of the Minister, make regulations

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 62(a) to (q) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the composition of committees established under subsection 19(1) and the rules, practices and procedures of those committees;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the duties and powers of the Registrar;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the investigation of complaints, the procedures of the Complaints Committee and the Discipline Committee and the hearing or other treatment of allegations of professional misconduct or incompetence and the actions taken in response to those allegations;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the definition of âprofessional misconductâ and âincompetenceâ for the purposes of this Act; and

Text.Ins: prescribing anything that is to be prescribed under this Act.

Section.Text: Section 64 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Text: tente dâobtenir une licence ou un permis en faisant sciemment une dÃ©claration fausse ou trompeuse sur un point important.

Section.Text: Section 68 of the Act is replaced by the following:

On application by the Association, if a court of competent jurisdiction is satisfied that a contravention of any provision of this Act, the regulations or the by-laws is being or is likely to be committed, the court may grant an injunction subject to any conditions that it considers appropriate, ordering any person to cease or refrain from any activity related to that contravention or ordering the person to take any measure that the court considers appropriate.
Section 71 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection 44(3), a notice or document required under any provision of this Act, the regulations or the by-laws to be served or delivered may be served personally or delivered electronically or by registered mail addressed to the person to whom the notice or document is to be given at the personâs last known address.

If a notice or document is delivered electronically or by registered mail, the delivery is deemed to have been made on the 10th day after the day on which it is sent unless the person to whom the notice or document is given establishes that the person, acting in good faith, through absence, illness or other cause beyond the personâs control, did not receive the notice or document, or did not receive the notice or document until a later date.

Section.Text: Section 72 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Any oath or affidavit mentioned in this Act may be taken before any judicial officer, notary public, commissioner for taking oaths or Canada Lands Surveyor.
Section 76 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every reference to the former Association in any deed, contract, agreement or other document executed or signed according to all the required formalities for their validity by the former Association shall, unless the context otherwise requires, be read as a reference to the new Association.
The following definitions apply in this section and sections 76 to 78.

Definition.Text: commencement dayâmeans the day on which section 28 comes into force.â(date de rÃ©fÃ©rence)
former Actâmeans the Canada Lands Surveyors Act as it read immediately before the commencement day.â(ancienne loi)
new Actâmeans the Canada Lands Surveyors Act as it reads on the commencement day.â(nouvelle loi)

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of the French version of the new Act, every permis, as defined in section 2 of the French version of the former Act, that is valid immediately before the commencement day is deemed to be a licence, as defined in section 2 of the French version of the new Act, and remains valid unless it is revoked or suspended before that day.

For the purposes of the French version of the new Act, every licence, as defined in section 2 of the French version of the former Act, that is valid immediately before the commencement day is deemed to be a permis, as defined in section 2 of the French version of the new Act, and remains valid unless it is revoked or suspended before that day.

Section.Text: For a period of two years after the commencement day, section 49.1 of the new Act does not apply to a person who holds a commission granted under the Canada Lands Surveys Act as it read immediately before March 18, 1999, who is not a member as defined in section 2 of the new Act. That person is deemed to be a Canada Lands Surveyor, as defined in section 2 of the new Act, during that two-year period.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2) and with the approval of the Minister of Natural Resources, the Council of the Association of Canada Lands Surveyors, within the meaning of the new Act, may make regulations that it considers necessary to provide for any transitional matter arising from the coming into force of any provision enacted by sections 28 to 74.

The Council of the Association of Canada Lands Surveyors, within the meaning of the new Act, may make by-laws respecting any matter under section 18 of the new Act that it considers necessary to provide for any transitional matter arising from the coming into force of any provision enacted by sections 28 to 74.
Sections 16 to 27 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Sections 28 to 74 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Heading.TitleText: Wild Animal and Plant Protection and Regulation of International and Interprovincial Trade Act

Subsection.Text: Subsection 6(1) of the Wild Animal and Plant Protection and Regulation of International and Interprovincial Trade Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to the regulations, no person shall import into Canada any animal or plant that was taken, or any animal or plant, or any part or derivative of an animal or plant, that was possessed, distributed or transported in contravention of any law of any foreign state.
Subsections 6(2) and (3) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Sous rÃ©serve des rÃ¨glements, il est interdit dâimporter au Canada ou dâexporter hors du Canada â sauf en vertu dâun permis dÃ©livrÃ© en vertu du paragraphe 10(1) et conformÃ©ment aux conditions qui sây rattachent â tout ou partie dâun animal, dâun vÃ©gÃ©tal ou dâun produit qui en provient.

Sous rÃ©serve des rÃ¨glements, il est interdit dâacheminer dâune province Ã lâautre â sauf en vertu dâun permis dÃ©livrÃ© en vertu du paragraphe 10(1) et conformÃ©ment aux conditions qui sây rattachent â tout ou partie dâun animal, dâun vÃ©gÃ©tal ou dâun produit qui en provient.

Section.Text: Section 7 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, if a licence issued by a competent authority in a province is required in order to transport any animal or plant, or any part or derivative of an animal or plant, out of the province, no person shall transport any animal, plant or part or derivative of an animal or plant from the province to another province except under and in accordance with such a licence.

Subject to the regulations, no person shall transport from a province to another province any animal or plant, or any part or derivative of an animal or plant, if the animal or plant was taken, or the animal, plant, part or derivative was possessed, distributed or transported, in contravention of any provincial Act or regulation.

Section.Text: The heading before section 10 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection 10(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Le ministre peut dÃ©livrer, sur demande et aux conditions quâil estime indiquÃ©es, un permis autorisant lâimportation, lâexportation ou lâacheminement interprovincial de tout ou partie dâun animal, dâun vÃ©gÃ©tal ou dâun produit qui en provient.
Subsection 10(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
AprÃ¨s avoir donnÃ© Ã lâintÃ©ressÃ© la possibilitÃ© de faire valoir ses observations, le ministre peut annuler ou suspendre le permis en cas de contravention Ã toute condition dont il est assorti.
Paragraph 21(1)(a) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: rÃ©gir la dÃ©livrance, le renouvellement, lâannulation ou la suspension des permis et prÃ©voir les cas de dispense;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 21(2) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 42(1) of the Species at Risk Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsections (2) and (3), the competent minister must include a proposed recovery strategy in the public registry within one year after the wildlife species is listed, in the case of a wildlife species listed as an endangered species, and within two years after the species is listed, in the case of a wildlife species listed as a threatened species or an extirpated species.
Subsection 42(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection (2), the competent minister must include a proposed recovery strategy in the public registry within one year after the wildlife species is listed, in the case of a wildlife species listed as an endangered species, and within two years after the species is listed, in the case of a wildlife species listed as a threatened species or an extirpated species.
Section 42 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

If a wildlife species is added to the List as the result of an assessment under section 130, the competent minister must include a proposed recovery strategy in the public registry within three years after the wildlife species is listed, in the case of a wildlife species listed as an endangered species, and within four years after the wildlife species is listed, in the case of a wildlife species listed as a threatened species or an extirpated species.
Subsection 42(3) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 68(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsections (2) and (2.1), the competent minister must include a proposed management plan in the public registry within three years after the wildlife species is listed as a species of special concern.
Subsection 68(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsection (2), the competent minister must include a proposed management plan in the public registry within three years after the wildlife species is listed as a species of special concern.
Section 68 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

If a wildlife species is added to the List as a species of special concern as the result of an assessment under section 130, the competent minister must include a proposed management plan in the public registry within five years after the wildlife species is listed.
Subsection 68(2.1) of the Act is repealed.
The heading before section 130 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Assessment of Wildlife Species Mentioned in Schedule 3

Subsection.Text: The heading before section 130 of the Act is repealed.
The portion of subsection 130(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
COSEWIC must assess the status of each wildlife species set out in Schedule 3, and, as part of the assessment, identify existing and potential threats to the species and
Subsection 130(1) of the Act is repealed.
Subsections 130(2) and (3) of the Act are repealed.
Subsection 130(4) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 130(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister after consultation with the competent minister or ministers, by order, extend the time provided for the assessment of any species set out in Schedule 3. The Minister must include a statement in the public registry setting out the reasons for the extension.
Subsections 130(5) and (6) of the Act are repealed.
Subsection 130(7) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Section 131 of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 131:

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, amend Schedule 3 by removing a species if COSEWIC has assessed the status of the species under section 130 or has determined that the species is not a wildlife species or a species at risk.

Subsection.Text: Section 131.1 of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Sections 132 and 133 of the Act are repealed.
Schedule 2 to the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Schedule 3 to the Act is replaced by the Schedule 3 set out in Schedule 1 to this Act.
Schedule 3 to the Act is repealed.
Sections 80 to 84 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsections 85(2) and (4), 86(2) and (4), 87(2) and 88(2), (4) and (6), section 89 and subsections 90(2) and 93(2) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The second paragraph of the preamble to the Agricultural Products Marketing Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: AND WHEREAS the legislatures of several of the provinces have enacted legislation respecting the marketing of agricultural products within the province;

Subsection.Text: The fourth paragraph of the preamble to the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: quâil convient de faciliter cette commercialisation en autorisant lâinstitution de redevances et prÃ©lÃ¨vements pour une meilleure rÃ©partition ou la pÃ©rÃ©quation, entre producteurs, des sommes rapportÃ©es par la commercialisation des produits,

Section.Text: Sections 1.1 to 4 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Text.Ins: The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: administrative bodyâmeans an administrative body referred to in subsection 3(3).â(organisme administratif)
agricultural product includes wood.â(produit agricole)
marketing boardâmeans a marketing board named in column 2 of the schedule.â(office de commercialisation)
Minister means the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food.â(ministre)
personâmeans an individual, corporation, partnership, trust, joint venture or unincorporated association or organization.â(personne)

Subsection.Text: A marketing board may, in relation to the marketing of an agricultural product named or described in column 3 of the schedule, exercise in the market set out in column 4 all or any powers like the powers that it may exercise under the law of a province.
Amarketing board has the power, in relation to the marketing of an agricultural product named or described in column 3 of the schedule in the market set out in column 4,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to establish and impose levies or charges and to collect them from persons that are engaged in the production or marketing of the whole or any part of the agricultural product;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of paragraph (a), to classify those persons into groups and to establish the levies or charges payable by the members of the different groups in different amounts; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to use the levies or charges for the purposes of the marketing board, including

Subparagraph.Text: the payment of expenses and losses resulting from the sale or disposal of the agricultural product, and
the equalization or adjustment among producers of the agricultural product of moneys realized from the sale of the product during any period or periods of time that the marketing board may determine.

Subsection.Text: An administrative body may, in relation to the marketing of an agricultural product named or described in column 3 of the schedule, exercise in the market set out in column 4 all or any powers like the powers â with respect to approvals, supervision, arbitrations or appeals â that it may exercise under the law of a province.

The powers granted under section 3 do not include any power that is exercised by the Canadian Dairy Commission under the Canadian Dairy Commission Act or by an agency established under the Farm Products Agencies Act.
The powers granted under section 3 may be exercised only with respect to persons that are situated within the province and to agricultural products that are produced within the province.

Despite subsection (2), a marketing board or an administrative body from the same province may exercise the powers granted to it under section 3 in relation to the marketing of milk only with respect to a person that has been granted a quota for the production of milk by the marketing board or administrative body and to milk produced under that quota, regardless of whether the person is situated, or the milk is produced, within or outside of the province.

For the purposes of subsection (3), milk means that agricultural product as it is described in column 3 of the schedule for each marketing board â or an administrative body from the same province â that is granted powers under section 3 in relation to the marketing of that agricultural product.

Section.Text: A levy or charge imposed by a marketing board under section 3constitutes a debt due to it and may be recovered by it in any court of competent jurisdiction.

Subsection.Text: A marketing board or administrative body must make accessible to the persons with respect to which it exercises the powers granted to it under section 3, through the Internet or by any other means that it considers appropriate, every requirement or other measure that it establishes in the exercise of such a power.
A requirement or other measure is not enforceable in respect of any period during which the requirement or measure is not made accessible in accordance with this section.

Section.Text: In the event of a conflict between a requirement or other measure established by a marketing board in the exercise of the powers granted to it under section 3 and a requirement or other measure established by an administrative body from the same province in the exercise of the powers granted to it under that section, the requirement or measure established by the marketing board prevails to the extent of the conflict.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by order, amend the schedule by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (2), adding, deleting or amending, in column 2, the name of a marketing board;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to a marketing board, adding, deleting or amending, in column 3, the name or description of an agricultural product; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to a marketing board, adding, deleting or amending, in column 4, a market.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may add the name of a marketing board to the schedule only if the board is authorized under the law of a province to exercise powers in relation to the marketing of an agricultural product within the province.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act.
The Act is amended by adding, after section 9, the schedule set out in Schedule 2 to this Act.
The Orders and Regulations set out in Schedule 3 to this Act are repealed.

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section and in sections 100 and 101.

Definition.Text: new Actâmeans the Agricultural Products Marketing Act as it reads on the day on which this section comes into force.â(nouvelle loi)

transitional instrumentsâmeans the Orders and Regulations set out in items 2, 6, 10, 11, 22, 25, 30, 32, 33, 35, 38 to 42, 44, 45, 48, 54, 66, 69, 71 to 76, 78, 81, 89, 90, 94, 97, 103 to 106, 108 to 111, 114, 115, 117 to 122, 124, 125, 129, 130, 132, 133, 141, 143, 144, 150 and 158 to 161 of Schedule 3 to this Act, as they read immediately before the day on which this section comes into force. â(textes transitoires)

Subsection.Text: Unless the context requires otherwise, the words and expressions used in sections 100 and 101 have the same meaning as in the new Act.
The transitional instruments continue to apply as though they were made or issued under the new Act.
