A levy or charge imposed by a marketing board under a transitional instrument on or after the day on which this section comes into force constitutes a debt due to it and may be recovered by it in any court of competent jurisdiction.
A transitional instrument ceases to apply on the earlier of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which the marketing board makes accessible to the persons with respect to which it exercises the powers granted to it under section 3 of the new Act, through the Internet or by any other means that it considers appropriate, a requirement or other measure in substitution for the transitional instrument, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day after the third anniversary of the day on which this section comes into force.

Section.Text: A quota for the production of milk granted under the Agricultural Products Marketing Act that is in force immediately before the day on which this section comes into force continues in force, until it is revoked, as though it were granted under the new Act.

Heading.Label: PART 5
Heading.TitleText: Regulatory Measures Respecting Canadian Food Inspection Agency

Subsection.Text: Subsection 5(1) of the Feeds Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b.1):

Paragraph.Label: (b.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the amendment of any of the conditions to which the registration or approval of feeds are subject by reason of subsection 5.31(2);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 5(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (h.2):

Paragraph.Label: (h.3)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the recognition of a system of any foreign state or subdivision of any foreign state relating to the safety of feeds;

Section.Text: The heading before section 5.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5.3:

Subsection.Text: If feed is approved by the Minister or feed is registered, the approval or registration is subject to the prescribed conditions.

The Minister may make an approval or registration subject to any additional conditions that the Minister considers appropriate, including conditions respecting the prevention of harm to human or animal health or to the environment.
A person who manufactures, sells or imports feed must comply with all the conditions to which the approval or registration of the feed is subject.

Section.Text: Subsection 9.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The notice must either be delivered personally to the owner or importer of the feed or to the person having possession, care or control of it or be sent to the owner, importer or person by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method.

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Fertilizers Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: biotechnologyâhas the meaning assigned by the regulations;â(biotechnologie)
novel supplementâmeansâ

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a supplement that is not registered and not exempt from registration, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a supplement that is derived through biotechnology and has a novel trait; (supplÃ©ment nouveau)

Definition.Text: novel traitâhas the meaning assigned by the regulations; (caractÃ¨re nouveau)â
releaseâmeans, except for the purposes of subsection 9(2), any discharge or emission of a supplement into the environment or exposure of a supplement to the environment;â(dissÃ©mination)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 3.4:
A person must not release a novel supplement, except in accordance with the regulations.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 5(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b.1):

Paragraph.Label: (b.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the amendment of any of the conditions to which the registration or approval of fertilizers or supplements are subject by reason of subsection 5.31(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b.3)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the amendment of any of the conditions to which the authorization to release a novel supplement is subject by reason of subsection 5.32(1);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 5(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (c.4):

Paragraph.Label: (c.5)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the release of novel supplements;

Paragraph.Label: (c.6)
Paragraph.Text: defining the terms âbiotechnologyâ and ânovel traitâ for the purposes of this Act;

Section.Text: The heading before section 5.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 5.3:

Section.MarginalNote: Fertilizers and supplements â prescribed conditions

Subsection.Text: If a fertilizer or supplement is approved by the Minister or a fertilizer or supplement is registered, the approval or registration is subject to the prescribed conditions.

The Minister may make an approval or registration subject to any additional conditions that the Minister considers appropriate, including conditions respecting the prevention of harm to human, animal or plant health or to the environment.
A person who sells or imports a fertilizer or supplement must comply with all the conditions to which the approval or registration of the fertilizer or supplement is subject.

If the Minister authorizes, under the regulations, a person to release a novel supplement, the Minister may make the authorization subject to any conditions that the Minister considers appropriate, including conditions respecting the prevention of harm to human, animal or plant health or to the environment.
A person that has received authorization from the Minister to release a novel supplement must comply with all the conditions to which the authorization is subject.

Section.Text: Subsection 9.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The notice must either be delivered personally to the owner or importer of the fertilizer or supplement or to the person having possession, care or control of it or be sent to the owner, importer or person by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method.

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Seeds Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: releaseâmeans, except for the purposes of subsection 8(2), any discharge or emission of seed into the environment or exposure of seed to the environment and includes the growing and field testing of plants;â(dissÃ©mination)

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 3.2:
A person must not release seed, except in accordance with the regulations.
Subsection (1) does not apply to seed described in section 108 of the Seeds Regulations.
Subsection 3.3(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection (1) does not apply to prescribed seed.
Paragraph 4(1)(a.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: providing for the determination of varietal purity of seed crops and, in particular, for any such determination to be made by the Canadian Seed Growersâ Association and any standards established by that Association to be used;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 4(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a.4):

Paragraph.Label: (a.5)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the release of seed;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 4(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (j.3):

Paragraph.Label: (j.4)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the recognition of a system of any foreign state or subdivision of any foreign state relating to the safety of seeds;

Section.Text: Subsection 8.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The notice must either be delivered personally to the owner or importer of the seed or to the person having possession, care or control of it or be sent to the owner, importer or person by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method.

Section.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Health of Animals Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: environmentâmeans the components of the Earth and includesâ

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: air, land and water,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all layers of the atmosphere,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: all organic and inorganic matter and living organisms, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the interacting natural systems that include components referred to in paragraphs (a) to (c); (environnement)

Definition.Text: releaseâmeans, except for the purposes of subsection 45(1), any discharge or emission of a veterinary biologic into the environment;â(dissÃ©mination)

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 11.1:
A person must not release a veterinary biologic, except in accordance with the regulations.
Subsection (1) does not apply to a veterinary biologic or a release described in subsection 120.2(2) of the Health of Animals Regulations.
Subsection 11.2(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection (1) does not apply to prescribed veterinary biologics.

Section.Text: Subsection 18(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The notice must either be delivered personally to the owner or importer of the animal or thing or to the person having possession, care or control of it or be sent to the owner, importer or person by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 21:
The Minister may approve programs developed for any of the following purposes by an entity other than the Canadian Food Inspection Agency:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: preventing the introduction of any vector, disease or toxic substance into Canada or into another country from Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: controlling or eradicating, or preventing the spread of, vectors, diseases or toxic substances; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: quarantining, segregating, treating or disposing of, or dealing generally with, animals or things that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are, or are suspected of being, affected or contaminated by a disease or toxic substance,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: have been in contact with or in close proximity to animals or things that were, or are suspected of having been, affected or contaminated by a disease or toxic substance at the time of contact or close proximity, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: are, or are suspected of being, vectors, the causative agents of disease or toxic substances.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 26(a) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of subparagraph (i) and adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: exists in the place or the part of the place, but also exists outside of that place or that part of the place under such conditions that, in the opinion of the inspector or officer, it is no longer necessary to prevent its spread outside of the place or the part of the place; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 26(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the infected place is located in an area that the Minister has declared to be a primary control zone in respect of that disease or toxic substance and, in the opinion of the inspector or officer, it is no longer necessary for that place to be constituted to be an infected place under either of those sections to prevent the spread of that disease or toxic substance outside of that place.

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 64(1)(f) of the French version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: f)
Paragraph.Text: contrÃ´ler ou Ã©liminer des vecteurs, des maladies et des substances toxiques ou en empÃªcher la propagation et prendre toute mesure â notamment lâisolation, la mise en quarantaine, le traitement ou la destruction â Ã lâÃ©gard de telles substances ainsi que des animaux ou choses quiÂ :

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 64(1)(s) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (s)
Paragraph.Text: prohibiting or regulating the importation, exportation, preparation, manufacturing, preserving, packaging, labelling, storing, testing, transportation, sale, conditions of sale, advertising for sale, use, release and disposal of veterinary biologics and regulating their purity, potency, efficacy and safety;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 64(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (s.1):

Paragraph.Label: (s.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the approval of programs for the purposes of section 21.1;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 64.1:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may make an interim order that contains any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under this Act if the Minister believes that immediate action is required to deal with a significant risk, direct or indirect, to human or animal health and safety or the environment.
An interim order has effect from the time that it is made but ceases to have effect on the earliest of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 14 days after the day on which it is made, unless it is approved by the Governor in Council,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which it is repealed,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which a regulation made under this Act that has the same effect as the interim order comes into force,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: one year after the interim order is made or any shorter period that may be specified in the interim order, unless it is extended by the Governor in Council, and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the day that is specified in the order of the Governor in Council, if the Governor in Council extends the interim order.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may extend the interim order for a period of no more than two years after the end of the applicable period referred to in paragraph (2)(d).

A person is not to be determined to have committed a violation, or to be convicted of an offence, that consists of a contravention of an interim order that, at the time of the alleged contravention, had not been published in the Canada Gazette unless it is proved that, at the time of the alleged contravention, the person had been notified of the interim order or reasonable steps had been taken to bring the purport of the interim order to the notice of those persons likely to be affected by it.
An interim order is exempt from the application of sections 3 and 9 of the Statutory Instruments Act.

For the purpose of any provision of this Act other than this section, any reference to regulations made under this Act includes interim orders, and any reference to a regulation made under a specified provision of this Act includes a reference to the portion of an interim order containing any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under the specified provision.

The Minister shall ensure that a copy of each interim order is tabled in each House of Parliament within 15 days after the day on which it is made. It is to be sent to the Clerk of the House if the House is not sitting.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 64.11:
Subject to the regulations, the Minister may renew, amend, suspend or revoke a licence, permit, approval or other document issued by the Minister.
Subsection 8(2) of the Plant Protection Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The notice must either be delivered personally to the owner or importer of the thing or to the person having possession, care or control of it or be sent to the owner, importer or person by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method.

Section.Text: The heading before section 47.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 47.2:
The Minister may, subject to the regulations, renew, amend, suspend or revoke a permit or other document issued by the Minister.
The Canadian Food Inspection Agency Act is amended by adding the following after section 11:

Subsection.Text: The Agency may administer and enforce this Act and any Acts or provisions that it administers or enforces by virtue of section 11 using electronic means, and the Minister and the President may exercise any power or perform any duty or function conferred on the Minister or President under this Act or any of those Acts or provisions using electronic means.

Any person who is, or who belongs to a class that is, designated by the Minister under this Act or any other Act or provision referred to in subsection (1) for the purpose of administering or enforcing this Act or any of those other Acts and any person who is, or who belongs to a class of persons that is, designated by the President under subsection 13(3) may, in the exercise of their powers or the performance of their duties and functions, use the electronic means that are made available to them or specified by whichever one designated them or the class to which they belong.

Any person to whom the Minister delegates powers, duties and functions under subsection 4(2) and any person to whom the President delegates powers, duties and functions under section 7 may exercise the powers or perform the duties and functions using the electronic means that are made available to them or specified by whichever one delegated those powers, duties and functions to them.

For greater certainty, an electronic system, including an automated system, may be used by the Minister and by the President to make a decision under this Act or any other Act or provision referred to in subsection (1), and, for the same purpose, by a designated person, or a person who belongs to a designated class, referred to in subsection (2) and a person to whom powers, duties and functions are delegated under subsection 4(2) or section 7, if the Minister or President makes the system available to the person or class of persons that the Minister or President designates or to the person to whom the Minister or President delegates powers, duties and functions.

Section.Text: If this Act or any Act or provision that the Agency administers or enforces by virtue of section 11 requires a signature or any information to be provided, a decision, application or request to be made, notice to be given or any other document to be submitted or otherwise provided or requires the original of a document to be submitted or otherwise provided, the electronic version of the signature, information, decision, application, request, notice or other document meets the requirement if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the electronic version is provided by the electronic means, including an electronic system, that are made available or specified by the Minister or President; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other requirements that may be provided for in regulations made under section 11.3 are met.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the application of section 11.1 and paragraph 11.2(b), including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the technology or format to be used, or the standards, specifications or processes to be followed, including for the making or verifying of an electronic signature and the manner in which it is to be used;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the date and time when, and the place where, an electronic version of a decision, application, request, notice or other document or of any information is deemed to be sent or received;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the circumstances in which a person who provides a signature or any information, makes a decision, application or request, gives notice or submits or otherwise provides any other document under this Act or any Act or provision that the Agency administers or enforces by virtue of section 11 is required to do so using electronic means, including an electronic system, or may do so using other means, and prescribing those means; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the circumstances in which the Minister or the President may authorize or require a person who provides a signature or any information, makes a decision, application or request, gives notice or submits or otherwise provides any other document under this Act or any Act or provision that the Agency administers or enforces by virtue of section 11 to do so using any means that the Minister or President specifies.

Subsection.Text: The regulations may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: require that payments that are to be made or evidence of payment that is to be provided under this Act or any Act or provision that the Agency administers or enforces by virtue of section 11 are to be made or provided by means of an electronic system;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: include provisions respecting such a system, respecting the circumstances in which those payments may be made or evidence of payments may be provided by other means and respecting those other means; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: include provisions respecting the date and time when, and the place where, an electronic payment or evidence of payment is deemed to be sent or received.

Section.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition food commodity in section 2 of the Safe Food for Canadians Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any food as defined in section 2 of the Food and Drugs Act, taking into account that that definition is subject to section 2.4 of that Act;

Section.Text: Subsection 32(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The notice must either be delivered personally to the owner or importer of the food commodity or to the person having possession, care or control of it or be sent to the owner, importer or person by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method.
Subsection 56(2) of the Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (c) and by replacing paragraph (d) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: one year after the interim order is made or any shorter period that may be specified in the interim order, unless it is extended by the Governor in Council, and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the day that is specified in the order of the Governor in Council, if the Governor in Council extends the interim order.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 56(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Governor in Council may extend the interim order for a period of no more than two years after the end of the applicable period referred to in paragraph (2)(d).

A person is not to be determined to have committed a violation, or to be found guilty of an offence, that consists of a contravention of an interim order that, at the time of the alleged contravention, had not been published in the Canada Gazette unless it is proved that, at the time of the alleged contravention,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person had been notified of the interim order; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: reasonable steps had been taken to bring the purport of the interim order to the notice of those persons likely to be affected by it.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 113(2) comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsection 117(2) and section 123 come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Sections 125 and 126 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The definitions environmental risk and health risk in subsection 2(1) of the Pest Control Products Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: environmental risk, in respect of a pest control product, means the possibility of harm to the environment, including its biological diversity, resulting from exposure to or use of the product, taking into account its conditions of registration or conditions of authorization or proposed conditions of registration or proposed conditions of authorization, as the case may be. (risque environnemental)

health risk, in respect of a pest control product, means the possibility of harm to human health resulting from exposure to or use of the product, taking into account its conditions of registration or conditions of authorization or proposed conditions of registration or proposed conditions of authorization, as the case may be. (risque sanitaire)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition conditions of registration in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any conditions specified by the Minister under paragraph 8(1)(a) or subsection 8(2) or when amending or renewing the registration of a pest control product under this Act; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition value in subsection 2(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: value, in respect of a pest control product, means the productâs actual or potential contribution to pest management, taking into account its conditions of registration or conditions of authorization or proposed conditions of registration or proposed conditions of authorization, as the case may be, and includes the productâsââ

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: conditions of authorization meansââ

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any conditions specified by the Minister under subsection 41(1.1) or when amending or renewing an authorization made under section 41; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any other requirements stated by this Act or the regulations to be conditions of authorization. (conditions dâautorisation)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition conditions of authorization in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any conditions specified by the Minister under subsection 41(1.1) or 41.02(2) or when amending or renewing an authorization made under section 41 or 41.02; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this Act, the health risks or environmental risks of a pest control product are acceptable if there is reasonable certainty that no harm to human health, future generations or the environment will result from exposure to or use of the product, taking into account its conditions of registration or proposed conditions of registration or conditions of authorization or proposed conditions of authorization, as the case may be.
Subsection 6(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

No person shall manufacture, possess, handle, store, transport, import, distribute or use a pest control product that is not registered under this Act, except as otherwise authorized under subsections 21(5), 41(1) or 41.02(1), section 48 or 51, any of sections 53 to 59 or the regulations.
Subsection 6(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Except as otherwise authorized under section 53.3 or 54, no person shall manufacture, import, export or distribute a pest control product that is referred to in an authorization under section 41 unless the conditions of authorization respecting the productâs composition and any other conditions of authorization have been complied with.

Except as otherwise authorized under section 53, 53.3 or 54, no person shall store, import, export or distribute a pest control product that is not packaged and labelled in accordance with the regulations and,

Paragraph.Text: if the product is registered, the conditions of registration; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the product is the subject of an authorization issued to a person under section 41, the conditions of authorization.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 6(2.1) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Except as otherwise authorized under section 53.3 or 54, no person shall manufacture, import, export or distribute a pest control product that is referred to in an authorization under section 41 or 41.02, unless the conditions of authorization respecting the productâs composition and any other conditions of authorization have been complied with.

Except as otherwise authorized under section 53, 53.3 or 54, no person shall store, import, export or distribute a pest control product that is not packaged and labelled in accordance with the regulations and,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the product is registered, the conditions of registration; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the product is the subject of an authorization issued to a person under section 41 or if the product is authorized under section 41.02, the conditions of authorization.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 6(5)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the product is registered or if it is the subject of an authorization under section 41, the directions on the label recorded in the Register, subject to the regulations.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 6(5)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the product is registered or if it is the subject of an authorization under section 41 or if it is authorized under section 41.02, the directions on the label recorded in the Register, subject to the regulations.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 6(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

No person shall package, label or advertise a pest control product in a way that is false, misleading or likely to create an erroneous impression regarding its character, value, quantity, composition, safety, registration or authorization.

Section.Text: The heading âApplications for Registration or Amendmentâ before section 7 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Applications for Registration, Amendment or Renewal

Section.Text: Subsection 7(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An application to register a pest control product or to amend or renew the productâs registration shall be made to the Minister in the form and manner directed by the Minister and shall include any information or other thing that is required by the regulations to accompany the application.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7:

Subsection.Text: On receiving an application under section 7, if the Minister determines that the pest control product meets the prescribed criteria referred to in paragraph 41.02(1)(a), the Minister may refuse the application and evaluate the product for an authorization under section 41.02.
If the Minister refuses the application and evaluates the product for an authorization under section 41.02, the Minister shall provide the applicant with written reasons.
The portion of subsection 8(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If the Minister considers that the health risks and environmental risks and the value of the pest control product are acceptable after any required evaluations and consultations have been completed, the Minister shall register the product or amend or renew its registration in accordance with the regulations, if any, by
Paragraph 8(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: specifying the period for which the registration or amended or renewed registration is valid, which period may be either finite or indefinite.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 8:

If the Minister determines that a registered pest control product meets the prescribed criteria referred to in paragraph 41.02(1)(a), the Minister may, at any time and with the written consent of the registrant, convert the productâs registration to an authorization under section 41.02, based on that prescribed criteria for the product or class of products.
The heading before section 41 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsections 41(1) to (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Authorization, amendment or renewal â person

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, on request and subject to subsections (1.1) to (1.4) and in accordance with the regulations, authorize a person or amend or renew an authorization for the person to manufacture, handle, store, transport, import, export, distribute, use or dispose of a pest control product or a class of pest control products.
The Minister may, in accordance with the regulations, if any, specify any conditions of authorization

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manufacture, handling, storage, transport, import, export, distribution, use or disposition of, including conditions of authorization relating to the composition of the product or class of products;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the recording, retaining and reporting to the Minister of information on sales of the product or the class of products within the time and in the form and manner directed by the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the reporting of information that relates to the health risks or environmental risks or, if applicable, the value of the product or class of products to the Minister within the time and in the form and manner directed by the Minister.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (1.1)(b) and (c) continue to apply, in accordance with the regulations, to a person authorized under this section whose authorization ceases to have effect.
The Minister may modify or delete any conditions of authorization if the Minister considers it necessary

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of addressing additional information related to a change in the productâs or class of productsâ health risks or environmental risks or, if applicable, value; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of clarifying, correcting or updating information relevant to the authorization.

Subsection.Text: Before issuing, amending or renewing the authorization, the Minister may consider if the value of the product or class of products is acceptable.

The Minister shall issue, amend or renew the authorization if the Minister considers that the manufacture, handling, storage, transportation, importation, distribution or use of the product or class of products for the specified purpose in accordance with any conditions of authorization specified does not pose health risks and environmental risks that are unacceptable and, if applicable, the value of the product or class of products is acceptable.

The Minister shall specify the period, in accordance with the regulations, if any, for which the authorization or amended or renewed authorization is to be valid, which period may be either finite or indefinite.

The Minister may, by delivering a notice in writing, require a person who makes a request under subsection (1) to provide the Minister with other information in support of the application within the time and in the form specified in the notice.
The Minister shall deny the request if the person who made the request under subsection (1) does not comply with the notice.

The Minister may suspend an authorization, if the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the conditions of authorization to which the authorization is subject have not been or will not be met and the Minister shall suspend an authorization if the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the continuance of the authorization poses unacceptable health risks or environmental risks.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 41:

Subsection.Text: If the Minister initiates a re-evaluation in respect of a registered active ingredient contained in a pest control product that is the subject of an authorization issued to a person under section 41, the Minister may include that pest control product in the re-evaluation.

If the Minister determines that the product is to be included in the re-evaluation, the provisions of this Act and regulations that apply to the re-evaluation of a registered active ingredient apply to the re-evaluation of the product with any necessary modifications.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 41.01:

Section.MarginalNote: Authorization, amendment or renewal â pest control product

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, subject to subsections (2) and (4) and in accordance with the regulations, authorize a pest control product or class of pest control products or amend or renew the authorization for the product, to be manufactured, handled, stored, transported, imported, exported, distributed, used or disposed of by any person, if the Minister considers

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the product or class of products meets the prescribed criteria;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the health risks and environmental risks of the product or class of products are acceptable; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the value of the product or class of products to be acceptable.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, in accordance with the regulations, if any, specify any conditions of authorization

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manufacture, handling, storage, transport, import, export, distribution, use or disposition of, including conditions of authorization relating to the composition of the product or class of products;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the recording, retaining and reporting to the Minister, information on sales of the product or the class of products within the time and in the form and manner directed by the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the reporting of any information that relates to the health risks or environmental risks or the value of the product or class of products to the Minister within the time and in the form and manner directed by the Minister.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (2)(b) and (c) continue to apply, in accordance with the regulations, to a person who was engaged in an activity referred to in subsection (1) in respect of a product or class of products whose authorization has ceased to have effect.
The Minister may modify or delete any conditions of authorization if the Minister considers it necessary

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of addressing additional information related to a change in the productâs or class of productsâ health risks or environmental risks or value; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of clarifying, correcting or updating information relevant to the authorization.

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall specify the period, in accordance with the regulations, if any, for which the authorization or amended or renewed authorization is to be valid, which period may be either finite or indefinite.
Despite subsection (1), the Minister may determine that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a product or class of products that meets the prescribed criteria for an authorization should be the subject of an application under section 7 for registration; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a product or class of products referred to in paragraph (a) is not to be the subject of a determination for an authorization or its amendment or renewal under this section.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may suspend an authorization if the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the conditions of authorization have not been or will not be met and the Minister shall suspend an authorization if the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that the continuance of the authorization poses unacceptable health risks or environmental risks.

If the Minister is considering issuing an authorization under this section, the Minister shall consult the public and federal and provincial government departments and agencies whose interests and concerns are affected by the federal regulatory system, on the authorization and any conditions of authorization, before making a decision to issue it.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Consultation â suspension and public interest
Subsection.Text: The Minister shall consult the public and federal and provincial government departments and agencies whose interests and concerns are affected by the federal regulatory system, before making a decision

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: about the reinstatement, amendment or cancellation of an authorization after its suspension; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: about any other matter in relation to an authorization if the Minister considers it in the public interest to do so.

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall invite any person to send written comments on the proposed decision and specify the period within which any comments are to be received.
The Minister shall consider any comments received before making a decision.
The Minister shall reinstate, amend or cancel the authorization referred to in paragraph (9)(a) after considering the comments that are received.
For greater certainty, an authorization that is issued, amended or renewed under this section is not a statutory instrument within the meaning of the Statutory Instruments Act.

If the Minister initiates a re-evaluation in respect of a registered active ingredient that is contained in a pest control product that has been authorized under section 41.02, the Minister may include that pest control product in the re-evaluation.

If the Minister determines that the product is to be included in the re-evaluation, the provisions of this Act and regulations that apply to the re-evaluation of the registered active ingredient apply to the re-evaluation of the product with any necessary modifications.
The portion of paragraph 42(2)(a) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for each application to register, amend or renew the registration of a pest control product,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 42(2)(c)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in support of an application for registration or for the amendment or renewal of a registration, or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 42(2)(n) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: authorizations under sections 33 and 41 and amendments and cancellations of authorizations under sections 34 and 41 and renewals of authorizations under section 41;

Paragraph.Label: (n.1)
Paragraph.Text: orders for recalls and for the taking of measures under this Act; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 42(2)(n) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: authorizations under sections 33, 41 and 41.02 and amendments and cancellations of authorizations under sections 34, 41 and 41.02 and renewals of authorizations under sections 41 and 41.02;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 43(1)(b)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: that the person does not intend to use the test data, or make the test data available to others, in order to register a pest control product in Canada or elsewhere or to amend or renew a registration.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 43(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: use the test data in order to register a pest control product in Canada or elsewhere, or to amend or renew a registration; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: make the test data available to others for the purpose of registering a pest control product in Canada or elsewhere, or of amending or renewing a registration.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 44:

TitleText.Ins: Orders for Recall and for Taking of Measures

Subsection.Text: If the Minister believes on reasonable grounds that a pest control product or a class of pest control products endangers human health or safety or the environment, the Minister may, by order, direct a registrant, person authorized under section 41 or person who manufactures, imports, exports or distributes the product or class of products to recall it.
The order must be provided in the form of a written notice and must include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the reasons for the recall; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the time and manner in which the recall is to be carried out.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, by order, direct a registrant, person authorized under section 41 or person who manufactures, imports, exports or distributes a pest control product or class of pest control products to take any measure referred to in subsection (2) if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister has made an order under section 44.1 with respect to the product or class of products; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister believes on reasonable grounds that the product is the subject of a measure or recall undertaken voluntarily by the registrant, person authorized under section 41 or person who manufactures, imports, exports or distributes the product or class of products.

Subsection.Text: The measures include stopping the manufacturing, handling, importing, exporting, storing, distributing, testing or transporting of the product or class of products or causing any of those activities to be stopped.
The order must be provided in the form of a written notice and must include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the reasons for the measure; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the time and manner in which the measure is to be carried out.

Section.MarginalNote: Recall or measures taken by Minister
Section.Text: If a person does not comply with an order made under section 44.1 or 44.2 within the time specified, the Minister may, on his or her own initiative and at that personâs expense, carry out the recall or measure required.
The Minister may designate any individual or class of individuals that are qualified as review officers for the purpose of reviewing an order under section 44.5.

Subsection.Text: Subject to any other provision of this section, an order that is made under section 44.1 or 44.2 shall be reviewed on the written request of the person who was ordered to recall a pest control product or class of pest control products or to take another measure â but only on grounds that involve questions of fact alone or questions of mixed law and fact â by a review officer other than the individual who made the order.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Contents of and time for making request
Subsection.Text: The written request must state the grounds for review and set out the evidence â including evidence that was not considered by the individual who made the order â that supports those grounds and the decision that is sought. It shall be provided to the Minister within seven days after the day on which the order was provided or, in the event of a serious and imminent danger to human health or safety or to the environment, any shorter period that may be specified in the order.
The review is not to be done if the request does not comply with subsection (2) or is frivolous, vexatious or not made in good faith.
The person who made the request shall, without delay, be notified in writing of the reasons for the refusal.
A review officer â other than the individual who made the order â may review an order, whether or not a request is made under subsection (1).
An order continues to apply during a review unless the review officer decides otherwise.
A review officer shall complete the review no later than 30 days after the day on which the request is provided to the Minister.
The review officer may extend the review period by no more than 30 days if they are of the opinion that more time is required to complete the review. They may extend the review period more than once.
If the review period is extended, the person who made the request shall, without delay, be notified in writing of the reasons for extending it.
On completion of a review, the review officer shall confirm, amend, cancel or terminate the order.

The person who made the request or, if there is no request, the person who was ordered to recall the pest control product or to take another measure shall, without delay, be notified in writing of the reasons for the review officerâs decision under subsection (10).
An order that is amended is subject to review under this section.
Every person who does not comply with an order made under subsection 44.1(1) is guilty of an offence.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Failure to take measures â offence
Subsection.Text: Every person who does not comply with an order made under subsection 44.2(1) is guilty of an offence.
Every person who commits an offence under subsection (1) or (2) is liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine of not more than $200,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than six months, or to both; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine of not more than $500,000 or to imprisonment for a term of not more than three years, or to both.

Subsection.Text: An order made under section 44.1 or 44.2 is not a regulation within the meaning of the Statutory Instruments Act.
A person shall not be convicted of an offence under subsection 44.6(1) or (2) unless the person was notified of the order.
Subparagraph 57(1)(b)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: manufacturing, handling, storing, transporting, importing, exporting, distributing or using a pest control product in accordance with the conditions of registration or conditions of authorization.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 57(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An order under subsection (1) shall be communicated by delivering a written notice to the registrant, the person authorized under section 41 or, as the case may be, to the person having possession, care or control of the pest control product, activity or thing that was involved in the contravention or the owner of that product or thing and the notice shall be accompanied by a statement of the reasons for the order.
Subsection 57(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An order under subsection (1) shall be communicated by delivering a written notice to the registrant, the person authorized under section 41, the person who manufactures, imports, exports or distributes a pest control product or class of pest control products under section 41.02 or, as the case may be, to the person having possession, care or control of the pest control product, activity or thing that was involved in the contravention or the owner of that product or thing and the notice shall be accompanied by a statement of the reasons for the order.
The portion of subsection 62(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

An applicant for registration of a pest control product or a person who requests an authorization under section 41, a registrant or a person authorized under section 41, who does not reside in Canada, shall
Subsections 62(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Any correspondence, notices or documents received by the representative designated under subsection (2) are deemed to have been received by the person referred to in that subsection who designated the representative.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Requirement to communicate through designated representative
Subsection.Text: The Minister may require a person referred to in subsection (2) to conduct any communications with the Minister through the designated representative of that person.
The portion of paragraph 67(1)(f) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the registration of pest control products, including the issuance, amendment, renewal, suspension or cancellation of registration, the addition, modification or deletion of conditions of registration and the types of registration for classes of products, and, for each type,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 67(1)(f)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the period or maximum period for which the registration, amended or renewed registration is valid, which periods may be either finite or indefinite;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 67(1)(g) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: respecting which requirements of the regulations are conditions of registration or conditions of authorization;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 67(1)(k) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: respecting an authorization under section 41, including the issuance, amendment, renewal, suspension or cancellation of the authorization, the addition, modification or deletion of conditions of authorization and the types of authorization for classes of products, and, for each type,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the period or maximum period for which the authorization or amended or renewed authorization is valid, which periods may be either finite or indefinite;

Paragraph.Label: (k.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting information that may be required by the Minister for the purposes of issuing, amending, renewing, suspending or cancelling an authorization under section 41, or adding, modifying or deleting any conditions of authorization;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 67(1)(k) and (k.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: respecting an authorization under section 41 or 41.02, including the issuance, amendment, renewal, suspension or cancellation of the authorization, the addition, modification or deletion of conditions of authorization and the types of authorization for classes of products, and, for each type,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the period or maximum period for which the authorization or amended or renewed authorization is valid, which periods may be either finite or indefinite;

Paragraph.Label: (k.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting information that may be required by the Minister for the purposes of issuing, amending, renewing, suspending or cancelling an authorization under section 41 or 41.02, or adding, modifying or deleting any conditions of authorization;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 67(1)(t) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (t)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the keeping of records by registrants, persons authorized under section 41 and persons who manufacture, handle, store, transport, import, export, distribute, use or dispose of pest control products, in relation to the products that they manufacture, handle, store, transport, import, export, distribute, use, or dispose of and the requirements for making those records available to the Minister;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 67(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (u):

Paragraph.Label: (u.1)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the recording, by a person authorized under section 41, of information on the sale of a pest control product or class of pest control products or of information that relates to the health risks or environmental risks or the value of the product or class of products, the retention and reporting to the Minister of such information by the person authorized under section 41 and by the person whose authorization under that section ceases to have effect and the use of such information by the Minister;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 67(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (u.1):

Paragraph.Label: (u.2)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the recording, by a person who manufactures, imports, exports and distributes a pest control product or class of pest control products that is authorized under section 41.02, of information on the sale of the product or class of products or of information that relates to the health risks or environmental risks or the value of the product or class of products, the retention and reporting to the Minister of such information by them and by the person who manufactured, imported, exported and distributed the product or class of products authorized under section 41.02 after the authorization ceases to have effect and the use of such information by the Minister;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 67(1)(w) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (w)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the inspection and operation of establishments in which pest control products are manufactured;

Section.Text: Unless the context otherwise requires, words and expressions used in sections 150 and 151 have the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Pest Control Products Act.

Section 7.1 of the Pest Control Products Act, as enacted by section 136 of this Act, also applies to an application for registration or amendment or renewal of registration that has not been decided by the Minister before the day on which that section 136 comes into force.

The Minister may include a pest control product that is authorized under section 41.02 of the Pest Control Products Act in a re-evaluation if, on the day on which section 142 of this Act comes into force, the registered active ingredient

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is subject to a re-evaluation that has already been initiated under section 16 of the Pest Control Products Act, as it read before the day on which section 142 of this Act came into force; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is contained in the authorized pest control product.

Section.Text: Subsections 132(5) and 133(1), (3) and (5), sections 136, 138 and 142 and subsections 143(4), 146(3) and 148(5) and (8) come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The Coastal Fisheries Protection Act is amended by adding the following after section 6:

Text.Ins: The licences and permits referred to in paragraph 6(a) are not statutory instruments for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 17(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (c), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes any term or condition of a licence or permit issued under this Act.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 18(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Every person who commits an offence under paragraph 17(1)(a), (b), (c) or (e) is liable
The portion of subsection 18(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Every person who commits an offence under paragraph 17(1)(d) is liable

Section.Text: Paragraph 86.2(1)(g) of the Fisheries Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the alleged offender and the Attorney General have entered into an alternative measures agreement;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 86.96:

For greater certainty, nothing in sections 86.1 to 86.96 limits a fishery officer, fishery guardian or peace officer from exercising their discretion with respect to the enforcement of this Act, prior to a charge being laid, including by referring a matter to a process based on restorative justice principles.

Paragraph 86.2(1)(g) of the Fisheries Act, as enacted by section 156, applies to every alleged offender who, on the day on which that section comes into force, has been provided with initial disclosure of the Crownâs evidence.

Heading.TitleText: Department of Citizenship and Immigration Act

Section.Text: The Department of Citizenship and Immigration Act is amended by adding the following after section 5.2:
In sections 5.4 to 5.7, personal information has the same meaning as in section 3 of the Privacy Act.

Subject to any regulations made under section 5.7, the Minister may, for the purpose of exercising their powers or performing their duties or functions under an Act of Parliament or another lawful authority, disclose within the Department any personal information under the control of the Department.

Subject to any regulations made under section 5.7, the Minister may, for the purpose of the administration or enforcement of an Act of Parliament or of the legislature of a province or for the purpose of exercising their powers or performing their duties or functions under another lawful authority, disclose to any department, ministry, body, office or agency of the federal government or of a provincial government or to a federal or provincial Crown corporation personal information that is under the control of the Department and that relates to any of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the identity of an individual and any changes to their identity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the status of an individual in Canada and any changes to their status;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the contents or status of any document issued to an individual by the Minister under a provision for which the Minister is responsible in an Act of Parliament or under another lawful authority, including information relating to the issuance, provision, renewal, restoration, validity, variance, withholding, refusal, termination, cancellation, correction, revocation, recall, suspension, recovery or loss of such a document.

Section.Text: For greater certainty, nothing in this Act affects any authority to disclose personal information under another Act of Parliament, under an Act of the legislature of a province, at common law or under the royal prerogative.
For the purposes of sections 5.4 and 5.5, the Governor in Council may make regulations respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure of personal information under the control of the Department, including conditions for or limits on disclosure and the specification of purposes for disclosure; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the meaning of terms used in those sections.

Section.Text: Subsection 150.1(1) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (d), by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure of information collected for the purposes of this Act to federal departments and agencies for the purposes of cooperation.

Section.Text: Subsection 164(1.1) of the Customs Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, make regulations for any of the following purposes:

Paragraph.Text: the uniform interpretation, application and administration of a protocol, chapter or provision â set out in column 2 of Part 5 of the schedule â in an agreement set out in column 1, and any other matters that may be agreed on from time to time by the parties to that agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the implementation of an agreement set out in column 1 of Part 5 of the schedule.

Section.Text: Section 161 comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
The Canada Transportation Act is amended by adding the following after section 49:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may make an interim order that contains any provision that may be contained in a regulation described in subsection (2), that provides that any requirement or condition set out in such a regulation does not apply or that provides for alternative requirements or conditions. The Minister may make an order only if the Minister considers that it is in the public interest, having regard to the purposes of the Act under which such a regulation may be made, and if the order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: implements an international standard, with any modifications that the Minister considers necessary; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ensures compliance with Canadaâs international obligations.

Subsection.Text: A regulation referred to in subsection (1) is a regulation that may be made by the Governor in Council or by the Minister under a provision of an Act of Parliament, if the Minister is responsible for that provision.
Before making an interim order, the Minister must consult with any person or organization that the Minister considers appropriate in the circumstances.
An interim order takes effect on the day that it is made or on any later day specified in the order and ceases to have effect on the earliest of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which it is repealed,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day before the day on which a regulation that has the same effect as the interim order comes into force, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: three years after the day on which it takes effect or any shorter period specified in it.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of administration and enforcement, an interim order is deemed to be a regulation described in subsection (2) that may contain the same provisions as the order or whose requirements or conditions are varied or rendered inapplicable by the order. The provisions of the order are subjected to all administration and enforcement provisions â including offence and punishment provisions â applicable to such a regulation.

An interim order may designate one or more of its provisions as subject to an administrative monetary penalty regime under the Act described in subsection (2) if that Act authorizes the designation of provisions of a regulation described in that subsection as being subject to the regime.

The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an interim order. However, the order must be made publicly available unless, in the opinion of the Minister, its publication is inappropriate due to exceptional circumstances, such as if the publication compromises public safety.

A person is not liable to be found guilty of an offence or subjected to an administrative sanction for a contravention of an interim order that, at the time of the alleged contravention, had not been published in the Canada Gazette unless it is proved that, at that time, the person had been notified of the order.

Provision.Text: 1âProcessing means the manufacture of grape products or juices, beverages, spirits or wines from grapes and includes bottling, distilling or fermenting with sugar or sulphur dioxide or any other chemical.
2âProcessor means a person engaged in the business of processing grapes.
3âProcessing means

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: canning, dehydrating, drying, freezing, pickling or processing a vegetable with sugar or sulphur dioxide or any other chemical or by heat;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: combining or mixing a vegetable with one or more other vegetables; or

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: entering into a contract for the purchase of vegetables for the purpose of performing on the vegetables any of the operations mentioned in paragraph (a) or (b).

4âProcessing means the manufacture of grape products or juices, beverages, spirits or wines from grapes and includes bottling, distilling or fermenting with sugar or sulphur dioxide or any other chemical.
5âProcessing means

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: canning, dehydrating, chipping, drying, freezing or processing with any chemical or by heat and combining or mixing potatoes with one or more other vegetables; or

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: entering into a contract for the purchase of potatoes for the purpose of performing on the potatoes any of the operations mentioned in paragraph (a).
6âWool means unwashed wool shorn from a sheep.
7âMarket in bulk means to sell, offer for sale or deliver a product, in barrels or otherwise in bulk, to a processor, a wholesaler or any other intermediary.
